title: Bun 从 Zig 到 Rust 的迁移已经实锤，下个版本可能成为最后一个 Zig 版本
content: Bun’s founders announced that version 1.3.14, scheduled for release tomorrow, will be the last release built with Zig if the Rust rewrite is merged. The new Rust code has passed all Bun test suites on Linux, Windows, and macOS, and is expected to fix about 200 GitHub issues. Compared to Zig, the Rust version shows no performance regressions and uses similar code, but benefits from Rust’s safer tooling to reduce crashes. No blog post has yet been published about the rewrite.

I typically invest in dividend‑paying, undervalued, and obscure stocks, avoiding those with high cyclicality even if their valuations seem moderate. My preference is to enter when a sector hits a low point—a range rather than a specific price—and hold for 2–4 years, watching it rise from neglect to popularity. I target top‑tier companies at that low, as weak competitors or heavily indebted firms may not survive the cycle. All markets, from finance to commodities, are cyclical, so respect them.

title: 湖南一中学给学生发「退步快学生」证明进行惩戒，怎样看待这种做法？可能对学生心理造成怎样的影响？
content: A secondary school in Yiyang, Hunan, issued paper certificates labeling fourth‑year students as “rapid regressors” because their scores fell by over 100 points in a recent exam. The practice sparked online backlash, with critics saying it harms students' self‑esteem. School officials acknowledged the issue, recalled the certificates, and provided psychological counseling to affected students. The incident highlights concerns over punitive academic labeling and its potential mental‑health impacts.

title: 退休生活中最常见的税务陷阱——以及如何规避
content: Common tax pitfalls retirees face include miscalculating required minimum distributions, overlooking state tax changes on withdrawals, double‑taxing pensions and Social Security, and failing to account for index‑fund timing rules. To avoid them, plan distributions to stay below threshold limits, stay updated on state legislation, treat sources of income appropriately for tax treatment, and consider tax‑efficient investment strategies. These steps help keep retirement income within manageable tax brackets.

content: Anthropic, founded in 2021, is nearing a $1 trillion valuation and may float an IPO that would surpass SpaceX. Its annual recurring revenue surged from $100 million in 2023 to $450 billion in five months of 2026—a 4,500‑fold increase. The company’s rapid growth is driven by two flagship AI products, Claude Code and Cowork, which embed AI into software development and business workflows. Founder Jack Clark forecasts that by 2028 AI may begin self‑improving, triggering an “intelligence explosion” that could reshape industry and economy.

title: Conway's Law and Cross-Hatching
content: Conway’s Law and Cross‑Hatching discusses how organizational structures influence the designs they produce, examining why systems often mirror the communication patterns of their creators. The piece emphasizes clear division of work to maintain maintainable and scalable software.

title: 中科大等 9 所大学明确不招文科生，所有专业均要求选考物理和化学，为啥会这样？高中生要「弃文从理」吗？
content: Nine universities, including the University of Science and Technology of China and several others, have announced that for 2026 admissions all programs require optional physics and chemistry. Their undergraduate intake will be limited to students who took these science subjects. The move aims to align with a greater demand for STEM talent and may signal a shift away from broad humanities offerings in new curricula. However, it does not necessarily mandate that high‑school students abandon humanities in favor of science.

title: Kimi与DeepSeek到底谁更值钱？
content: Kimi and DeepSeek are China’s leading open‑source large‑model companies, competing yet collaborating. Kimi raised ~US$2 billion recently, valued at US$20 billion, and has $200 million annual recurring revenue, backed by Alibaba and Tencent’s cloud resources. DeepSeek, once self‑funded, is now seeking US$50 billion, valuing it at about US$51 billion—over two‑and‑a‑half times Kimi’s valuation—backed by state investment funds and its own founder’s capital. Both firms share technology (e.g., Muon optimizer, MLA) and push large‑parameter models, but Kimi focuses on commercial products while DeepSeek prioritizes ecosystem spread and future monetisation.

title: 接手王思聪公司后，何猷君豪赌海南

The property, intended for an esports‑sport‑tourism complex, requires a minimum 1.4 billion‑yuan investment and was won by Hainan Xingju Jiayuan Real Estate, a vehicle controlled by Younou’s companies. This acquisition follows his takeover of Wang Sicong’s Huanzhu Commercial Management and marks a strategic expansion of his esports‑tourism empire in Hainan’s free‑trade zone.

title: 黄仁勋致 2026 届毕业生：别慌，AI 把所有人拉回同一起跑线 | 附演讲全文
content: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang delivered an inspiring speech at Carnegie Mellon’s 2026 graduation, urging graduates not to panic about AI’s disruptive impact. He recounts his own humble beginnings and challenges building Nvidia, emphasizing that AI reshapes work rather than replaces it. Huang stresses the importance of responsible AI development, inclusive policy, and the new industrial opportunities AI creates. He calls on students to embrace this moment, use AI to expand human capability, and contribute to a future where technology benefits all.

title: 阿里或将整合千问与淘宝，打造 AI 对话式购物，你会把购物交给 AI 吗？
content: Alibaba plans to integrate its AI platform Qianwen with Taobao, replacing traditional keyword search with conversational shopping. Customers will use Qianwen’s app to chat with an AI assistant, which can browse more than 4 billion Taobao and Tmall items, compare prices, place orders, manage logistics, and offer after‑sales support. The assistant will recommend products based on past orders and preferences, and Taobao will also feature AI tools like virtual try‑on and price‑trend tracking. This move highlights China’s deeper AI integration in e‑commerce compared to more fragmented global platforms.

title: 内存正在毁掉一切
content: The article argues that rising memory chip costs, driven by AI’s demand, are disrupting not only tech companies but everyday life. Smartphone makers must raise prices and cut models, affecting consumers and secondary markets. Apple and Microsoft trim high‑memory options, while Windows PCs suffer sales. Even gaming consoles, cars, and crime patterns shift toward memory theft. In South Korea, memory giants’ profit sharing fuels employee wealth and workplace conflict. The piece concludes that memory inflation is eroding the tech industry's gains, with uncertainty about how long this distortion will last.

title: I'm going back to writing code by hand
content: The author reflects on returning to writing code by hand, citing a desire for deeper focus, improved understanding of algorithms, and less reliance on auto‑generation tools. They note that manual coding promotes mental practice, reduces cognitive overload from IDE shortcuts, and helps maintain clarity in complex projects. The article acknowledges potential drawbacks such as slower development and increased typing effort, but argues that the trade‑off results in cleaner, more maintainable code.

title: Make America AI ready: Strengths, weaknesses, and recommendations
content: The article evaluates the United States’ preparedness for artificial intelligence, outlining its strong points—such as advanced research infrastructure and supportive policy frameworks—while highlighting significant gaps in workforce skills, data governance, and public trust. It recommends targeted investments in education, clearer regulatory standards, and enhanced collaboration among industry, academia, and government to ensure AI benefits are realized safely and equitably.

title: 智谱GLM-5V-Turbo“擦枪走火”，国产多模态智能体战争一触即发
content: Zhipu AI’s GLM‑5V‑Turbo marks a shift toward native multimodal agents that perceive and interact with user interfaces. The model integrates a custom CogViT vision encoder, a token‑prediction architecture to curb memory costs, and multi‑task reinforcement learning across 30+ tasks. It moves beyond text‑only SaaS, enabling end‑to‑end planning, multimodal reading, and structured output, while stressing that model capacity depends on integration with external toolkits. The release also offers lessons on perception, layered training, and evaluation challenges for AI agents.

title: 互联网巨头的用人账：130万人、几千亿薪酬，流向哪里？
content: The 2025 annual reports of China’s top ten internet giants reveal a shift from hiring to reskilling. Most companies reduced overall staff, concentrating workers in R&D, AI, and core operations while scaling down non‑core roles. Compensation now favors talent who drive AI and technology projects, with large bonuses and equity. Capital spending also rises, especially for AI infrastructure, but varies—Alibaba and Baidu bet heavily on hardware, while Tencent, NetEase, and others focus on human capital. The trend shows firms using fewer people to achieve higher efficiency.

title: 75亿，一个网红独角兽破产
content: Self‑Hotpot, a fast‑growing Chinese self‑heat instant‑food brand that once reached a 7.5‑billion‑cny valuation, has gone bankrupt. It rode a wave of boom‑stage new‑consumer hype and heavy marketing to build a 10‑billion‑cny revenue in 2020, but was over‑spent and had low repeat sales. Reduced marketing costs in 2022 yielded a brief profit, yet shifting investor focus to hard‑tech left the company cash‑constrained, leading to its May 2024 liquidation.

title: 日本平价咖喱午餐面临“三难困境”
content: Japan’s affordable curry lunch dishes face an “impossible trinity,” where producers struggle to balance low cost, high quality, and healthy options. The market’s limited appetite for cheaper meals clashes with rising ingredient prices, health‑conscious consumer demands, and industry pressure to maintain consistent flavor and quality. This dilemma hampers mass production efforts and forces brands to rethink pricing, sourcing, and product positioning.

The author calls for developers and policymakers to prioritize local AI solutions to ensure safer, faster, and more autonomous digital interactions.

content: Advice for young people: To avoid pointless setbacks, consciously change cities, social circles, and personal connections. Staying in a stagnant, conservative environment with narrow‑minded, complacent people dulls ambition and limits perspective. Thrive by moving to a progressive city that embraces emerging industries, leaving behind unambitious circles, and distancing from self‑demoralizing individuals. Your ultimate success depends less on IQ or effort and more on the quality of your surroundings. Choose fertile environments, supportive networks, and like‑minded peers to shorten life’s detours.

title: 压缩上下文居然吃不到缓存？告诉我不是最后一个知道的
content: The developer built a transfer point called “metapi” for a Copilot‑harvesting environment to switch middle stations without reconfiguring clients. Logging showed most requests were cached, but one request bypassed the cache and cost approximately ten times more. Investigation revealed the issue stemmed from context compression; compressed‑context requests contain different commands, preventing subsequent content from being cached. The flaw is thus not the transfer station itself but the compression process, which the author now recognizes as a costly mistake.
